creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) The White House is asking OpenAI to slow roll the release of its new model over safety concerns
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: penAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of to the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.
contentSnippet: penAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of to the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.

creator: Marina Temkin
title: (No AI) Patronus AI lands $50M to build ‘digital worlds’ that stress-test AI agents

content: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by former Meta AI researchers, is experiencing nearly insatiable demand, its investor says.

title: (No AI) OpenAI will delay GPT-5.6 after Trump administration request
author: Hayden Field
content:
![Sam Altman with OpenAI logo on green background.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/STK201_SAM_ALTMAN_CVIRGINIA2A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The Trump administration, apprehensive of potential security issues, has [reportedly](https://www.theinformation.com/articles/trump-administration-asks-openai-stagger-release-new-model-security-concerns) asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its next big-ticket model, GPT-5.6.

_The Information_ reported that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told employees Wednesday in a company Q&A that it would release GPT-5.6 in limited preview form - granting access only to a small group of enterprise customers - in compliance with a request from the federal government. During that preview period, the Trump administration itself would reportedly approve access for customers on a case-by-case basis.

It's a more favorable deal than the Trump administration gave OpenAI rival Anthropic …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/957372/openai-will-delay-gpt-5-6-after-trump-administration-request)
contentSnippet:
The Trump administration, apprehensive of potential security issues, has reportedly asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its next big-ticket model, GPT-5.6. 
The Information reported that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told employees Wednesday in a company Q&A that it would release GPT-5.6 in limited preview form - granting access only to a small group of enterprise customers - in compliance with a request from the federal government. During that preview period, the Trump administration itself would reportedly approve access for customers on a case-by-case basis.
It's a more favorable deal than the Trump administration gave OpenAI rival Anthropic …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The Information reported that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told employees Wednesday in a company Q&A that it would release GPT-5.6 in limited preview form - granting access only to a small group of […]

title: (No AI) Ford had to hire back former engineers to fix mistakes made by its automated systems
author: Andrew J. Hawkins
content:
![The Ford logo on a graphic blue and white background.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/08/acastro_STK056_02.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

To celebrate its new status as No. 1 in JD Power's initial quality ranking among mainstream automakers, Ford is opening up about the challenges it has faced in recent years, especially around its reliance on automated systems in production and design. It turns out that those automated systems were not as robust as previously assumed, requiring Ford to hire experienced technicians - sometimes bringing back former employees - to correct errors made by the company's robots.

In Ford's view, AI is both powerful and prone to pitfalls. Its effectiveness depends entirely on the quality of the data used to train the AI models. In addition, the auto …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/transportation/956316/ford-quality-jd-power-ranking-ai-automated-mistakes)

In Ford's view, AI is both powerful and prone to pitfalls. Its effectiveness depends entirely on the quality of the data used to train the AI models. In addition, the auto …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Maxwell Zeff
title: (No AI) Anthropic Thinks Its Own Success Is Key to Making AI Safe
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff
content: Anthropic's critics argue it's rapidly accumulating power. The company says that's what responsible AI development looks like.
contentSnippet: Anthropic's critics argue it's rapidly accumulating power. The company says that's what responsible AI development looks like.

creator: Brian Barrett, Zoë Schiffer, Leah Feiger
title: (No AI) Why Amazon Dropped Its OpenAI Movie, Data Center Workers Fight Back, and Meta Leaks Employee Data
dc:creator: Brian Barrett, Zoë Schiffer, Leah Feiger
content: The decision by Amazon-owned MGM Studios to drop the OpenAI movie is just part of AI and film industries becoming increasingly intertwined. On Uncanny Valley, we look at where this is all headed.
contentSnippet: The decision by Amazon-owned MGM Studios to drop the OpenAI movie is just part of AI and film industries becoming increasingly intertwined. On Uncanny Valley, we look at where this is all headed.
